data.title: What's Going On With Profusa Stock Tuesday?
data.description:
Profusa, Inc. (NASDAQ:PFSA) shares tumbled during Tuesday's trading session.

Strategic Expansion Into Molecular Diagnostics

On Monday, Profusa said it signed a Letter of Intent to acquire BioInsights' PanOmics multi-omics diagnostics platform in an all-equity deal valued at about $30 million, marking its entry into molecular diagnostics.

The company said the platform will complement its Lumee real-time biochemistry monitoring system and strengthen its push into precision medicine, including a collaboration with Mayo Clinic focused on pancreatic cancer.

Deal Terms And Commercial Opportunity

Under the proposed terms, Profusa would gain exclusive rights to the platform, while BioInsights would provide clinical samples for validation and receive a 3% royalty on net revenue. The deal is subject to due diligence, shareholder approval, and other customary conditions.

Profusa said the integration could accelerate the development of lab-developed tests and support a scalable diagnostics and monitoring business. Initial commercialization efforts will target pancreatic cancer, with more than 13,000 annual surgeries in the U.S. and an estimated $26 million market opportunity.

Company Overview

Profusa is a clinical-stage digital health and medical technology company focused on developing biosensing solutions to improve health outcomes across a range of diseases and conditions.

Its core idea is bioengineered sensors designed to integrate with the body and continuously transmit clinical-grade data for personal and medical use.

Lumee Platform And Multi-Omics Strategy

The company's first offering in the European Union, the Lumee Oxygen Platform, is built to report tissue oxygen levels at different regions of interest, both acutely and long-term.

In that context, adding BioInsights' PanOmics platform is meant to broaden Profusa from sensing into multi-omics diagnostics, pairing real-time monitoring with lab-developed tests and clinically annotated samples.

Profusa Stock Price Movement

PFSA Price Action: Profusa shares were down 40.27% at $1.10 at the time of publication on Tuesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo by Aunt Spray via Shutterstock

data.title: What's Going On With Pharma Company PRF Technologies On Tuesday?
data.description:
2025 Key Events

PRF's highlighted a majority investment in Boston-based LayerBio in August 2025, adding the OcuRing-K program and expanding its ocular drug-delivery focus beyond post-operative pain.

PRF Technologies completed the acquisition of DeepSolar, entering the AI-driven solar analytics market and establishing a second high-growth business platform.

The company ended 2025 with around $4.1 million in cash, cash equivalents, short-term deposits, and restricted cash, and positive working capital of about $2.0 million.

PRFX Technical Levels: Key Support and Resistance to Watch

The broader market is trading lower today, with SPY down 0.49% and QQQ down 0.61%. Market breadth is weak (1 sector advancing, 10 declining), and that risk-off tone can amplify moves in smaller, higher-volatility names like PRFX.

At $2.30, PRFX is trading 4% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA), the stock's average price over the last 20 sessions, which suggests short-term momentum is slipping again. It's also trading 37.3% below its 100-day SMA, which indicates the intermediate trend is still pointed down.

The moving average convergence divergence (MACD), a trend/momentum measure, shows MACD at -0.2167 versus a signal line at -0.2534, and that "MACD above signal" setup suggests downside pressure has eased versus the prior pace. Even so, the 20-day SMA sitting below the 50-day SMA and the 50-day below the 200-day keeps the longer trend structure bearish.

On a longer lens, the stock is down 76.25% over the past 12 months, a backward-looking read that matches the steep gap to the 200-day averages. PRFX is also much closer to its 52-week low of $1.80 than its $17.95 high, which is consistent with a market still treating rallies as fragile.

  • Key Resistance: $2.50 — a round-number area where rebounds often stall first
  • Key Support: $2.00 — a psychological level that can attract dip-buying interest

PRF Technologies's 2026 Roadmap: Robotaxis, Optimus, and EV Scale

PRF Technologies is a diversified platform spanning specialty pharmaceuticals, drug-delivery technologies, and AI-driven renewable-energy analytics. Its pharma work leans on reformulation and sustained-release delivery, while its Solar segment focuses on building intellectual property and selling related services.

PRF Technologies is set to report earnings on April 21, 2026 (estimated).

PRFX Stock Price Activity: PRF Technologies shares were trading at $2.30 at the time of publication on Tuesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo via Shutterstock

data.title: As Medicaid Cuts Push 446 Hospitals To The Brink, Mark Cuban Says Their Spending And Overpaying Is The Real Problem. 'Prove Me Wrong'
data.description:
Hundreds of hospitals across the U.S. are now at risk of closing or cutting services following sweeping Medicaid cuts, but billionaire investor Mark Cuban says the real issue isn't just government policy; it's how hospitals spend their money.

Don't Miss:

Hospitals Under Pressure From Medicaid Cuts

The report says that reduced Medicaid funding is pushing already fragile hospitals closer to the edge. Facilities that rely heavily on Medicaid, and are already operating at a loss, are now facing even tighter margins.

The consequences are already showing up. Hospitals have begun laying off staff, shutting down clinics and cutting key services, especially obstetrics and maternal care. In some cases, leaders say they have no choice because reimbursement rates don't cover the cost of care.

The impact goes beyond rural areas. While rural hospitals remain vulnerable, about 60% of the at-risk hospitals are in urban areas, showing the strain is widespread.

Mark Cuban Calls Out Hospital Spending

“Why aren't any of these at risk hospitals publishing their full accounting so everyone can see where they spend their money?” Cuban wrote.

Trending: Avoid the #1 Investing Mistake: How Your ‘Safe' Holdings Could Be Costing You Big Time

Based on his experience reviewing hospitals for potential investments, Cuban said many facilities are spending heavily on consultants and unnecessary fees.

“All but one group of hospitals that I have looked at potentially investing in, spend so much on consultants and fees that it's no wonder they are at risk,” he said.

He also criticized how hospitals purchase supplies, saying they routinely overpay.

“I have NEVER seen an industry that is worse than hospitals when it comes to buying medications and items like implants, screws, other devices,” Cuban added. “They overpay for everything.” 

Even when presented with ways to cut costs, Cuban said hospitals resist change internally.

“When you show them how to save money, their ‘supply chain' employees resist any change,” he wrote. “They are so set in their ways, it's a shock more don't go out of business.”

He ended the post with a challenge: “Prove me wrong.”

A Debate Over What's Really Driving The Crisis

Not everyone agrees with Cuban's take. Some responses argued that hospitals are trapped in a broken system, losing money on Medicare and Medicaid patients while still being required to provide around-the-clock care and treat uninsured individuals.

Cuban pushed back, asking directly, “Why are they losing on Medicare and Medicaid?”

The report itself suggests multiple factors are at play. Medicaid cuts are a big factor, but hospitals are also dealing with staff shortages, fewer patients coming in and day-to-day inefficiencies.

Still, the stakes are high. Many of the hospitals at risk are the only providers in their communities or serve large low-income populations. If they close or cut services, access to care could shrink dramatically for millions of Americans.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Alphabet Stock Edges Higher Tuesday: What's Going On?
data.description:
• Alphabet stock is trading at elevated levels. What’s the outlook for GOOGL shares?

Lower-Cost AI Tools Could Help Deepen Developer Adoption

Alphabet's push to lower AI costs also follows last week's on-device AI rollout, with DeepMind's Gemma 4 models supporting 140+ languages and designed to run offline on edge hardware, a theme that reinforces developer stickiness across the stack via run directly on devices.

Google introduced Veo 3.1 Lite, a lower-cost AI video model for developers that runs at the same speed as Veo 3.1 Fast but at less than half the cost, with text-to-video and image-to-video support in 720p and 1080p.

The model is available starting today via the paid tier of the Gemini API and Google AI Studio, with clip lengths of 4, 6, or 8 seconds and pricing that scales by duration.

Google also said it will cut prices for Veo 3.1 Fast on April 7, reinforcing a broader push to lower AI tool costs and pull more developers into its ecosystem. The backdrop is also being helped by easing geopolitical-risk chatter, even as today's index-level action remains broadly negative.

Cost Discipline And AI Spend Trends Stay In Focus

The broader market is also sliding, with the S&P 500 down 0.69% and the Nasdaq down 0.92%.

Google Services Still Drives The Core Revenue Story

Alphabet is a holding company that wholly owns internet giant Google, and it still gets slightly less than 90% of its revenue from Google services—mostly advertising. That makes product decisions that increase usage and developer activity especially important, because they can strengthen the ecosystem that ultimately supports ad demand and subscriptions.

Next Earnings Report Marks Key Catalyst Ahead

Looking further out, the next major catalyst for the stock arrives with the April 23 (estimated) earnings report.

  • EPS Estimate: $2.67 (Down from $2.81 YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $100.76 Billion (Up from $90.23 Billion YoY)
  • Valuation: P/E of 27.5x (Indicates premium valuation relative to peers)

Alphabet Holds Buy Rating As Targets Move Higher

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $274.71. Recent analyst moves include:

  • UBS: Neutral (Raises Target to $348.00) (Feb. 12)
  • Scotiabank: Sector Outperform (Raises Target to $400.00) (Feb. 5)
  • JP Morgan: Overweight (Raises Target to $395.00) (Feb. 5)

Alphabet Shares Fall Slightly Tuesday

GOOG Price Action: Alphabet shares were up 0.28% at $298.50 at the time of publication on Tuesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: House Democrats Ask CFTC Why It Hasn't Cracked Down On Offshore Prediction Market War Bets
data.description:
Seven House Democrats sent a letter Monday to Commodity Futures Trading Commission Chair Michael Selig, asking why the agency has not acted against offshore war bets tied to U.S. military actions in Venezuela and Iran.

The letter, led by Reps. Jim McGovern and Seth Moulton, cites “high-profile instances of alleged insider trading” on platforms like Polymarket and Kalshi, and argues the CFTC already has authority under the Commodity Exchange Act to act. They want to know why it hasn’t.

Polymarket is an offshore company, but the letter points out that the Commodity Exchange Act allows the agency to regulate when "swap activities outside the United States have a direct and significant connection with activities in, or effect on, commerce of the United States."

The Trump Family Question

The letter also asks Selig whether the agency is aware of “conflicts of interest between major market participants and family members of Executive Branch officials.”

Donald Trump Jr. serves as an investor and unpaid advisor to Polymarket and a strategic advisor to Kalshi. The Trump family’s social media company has separately announced its own prediction market platform, Truth Predict.

Lawmakers have requested a response by April 15.

A Messy Week For The CFTC

The letter lands as the regulator is fighting on multiple fronts.

On Monday, a federal appeals court ruled New Jersey gaming regulators cannot bar Kalshi from offering sports event contracts, a win Selig called validation of federal preemption.

A CFTC crackdown on offshore war bets could signal broader regulatory pressure on the industry, or simply hand incumbents more ammunition in their fight to slow Kalshi and Polymarket’s expansion.

Image: Shutterstock
